findings.issue: Post-OAuth sign-in can still fail on the documented split frontend/API origin: OAuth and invitation calls honor VITE_API_BASE_URL, and .env.example sets it to a backend origin, but ProfileProvider validates the newly stored access_token with a hard-coded fetch('/v1/profile'). When the remote frontend is served from a non-loopback dev origin such as :3002 while the API is on :3000, the callback can redeem successfully and store localStorage.access_token, then /nodes immediately loads ProfileProvider against the frontend origin instead of the API origin and AuthGuard treats the user as signed out.
findings.remediation: Have ProfileProvider call profileApi.get() or otherwise use the same VITE_API_BASE_URL-aware API client used by the rest of remote-frontend; add a test that stubs VITE_API_BASE_URL/split-origin behavior and asserts profile validation fetches the configured API base after OAuth.
